sections.label: Section 7.1
sections.original: Forte sub arguta consederat ilice Daphnis ,
sections.translation: daphnis beneath a rustling ilex-tree

sections.label: Section 7.2
sections.original: compulerantque greges Corydon et Thyrsis in unum ,
sections.translation: had sat him down ; Thyrsis and Corydon

sections.label: Section 7.3
sections.original: Trhyrsis ovis , Corydon distentas lacte capellas ,
sections.translation: had gathered in the flock , Thyrsis the sheep ,

sections.label: Section 7.4
sections.original: ambo florentes aetatibus , Arcades ambo ,
sections.translation: and Corydon the she-goats swollen with milk —

sections.label: Section 7.5
sections.original: et cantare pares , et respondere parati .
sections.translation: both in the flower of age , Arcadians both ,

sections.label: Section 7.6
sections.original: Huc mihi , dum teneras defendo a frigore myrtos ,
sections.translation: ready to sing , and in like strain reply .

sections.label: Section 7.7
sections.original: vir gregis ipse caper deerraverat ; atque ego Daphnim
sections.translation: Hither had strayed , while from the frost I fend

sections.label: Section 7.8
sections.original: aspicio . Ille ubi me contra videt : “ Ocius ” inquit
sections.translation: my tender myrtles , the he-goat himself ,

sections.label: Section 7.9
sections.original: “ huc ades , O Meliboee , caper tibi salvus et haedi ;
sections.translation: lord of the flock ; when Daphnis I espy !

sections.label: Section 7.10
sections.original: et , si quid cessare potes , requiesce sub umbra .
sections.translation: Soon as he saw me , “ Hither haste ,” he cried ,

sections.label: Section 7.11
sections.original: huc ipsi potum venient per prata iuvenci ,
sections.translation: “ O Meliboeus ! goat and kids are safe ;

sections.label: Section 7.12
sections.original: hic viridis tenera praetexit arundine ripas
sections.translation: and , if you have an idle hour to spare ,

sections.label: Section 7.13
sections.original: Mincius , eque sacra resonant examina quercu .”
sections.translation: rest here beneath the shade . Hither the steers

sections.label: Section 7.14
sections.original: Quid facerem ? Neque ego Alcippen , nec Phyllida habebam ,
sections.translation: will through the meadows , of their own free will ,

sections.label: Section 7.15
sections.original: depulsos a lacte domi quae clauderet agnos ,
sections.translation: untended come to drink . Here Mincius hath

sections.label: Section 7.16
sections.original: et certamen erat , Corydon cum Thyrside , magnum .
sections.translation: with tender rushes rimmed his verdant banks ,

sections.label: Section 7.17
sections.original: posthabui tamen illorum mea seria ludo :
sections.translation: and from yon sacred oak with busy hum

sections.label: Section 7.18
sections.original: alternis igitur contendere versibus ambo
sections.translation: the bees are swarming .” What was I to do ?

sections.label: Section 7.19
sections.original: coepere ; alternos Musae meminisse volebant .
sections.translation: No Phyllis or Alcippe left at home

sections.label: Section 7.20
sections.original: hos Corydon , illos referebat in ordine Thyrsis .
sections.translation: had I , to shelter my new-weaned lambs ,

sections.label: Section 7.21
sections.original: Nymphae , noster amor , Libethrides , aut mihi carmen ,
sections.translation: and no slight matter was a singing-bout

sections.label: Section 7.22
sections.original: quale meo Codro , concedite : proxima Phoebi
sections.translation: ' twixt Corydon and Thyrsis . Howsoe ' er ,

sections.label: Section 7.23
sections.original: versibus ille facit ; aut , si non possumus omnes ,
sections.translation: I let my business wait upon their sport .

sections.label: Section 7.24
sections.original: hic arguta sacra pendebit fistula pinu .
sections.translation: So they began to sing , voice answering voice

sections.label: Section 7.25
sections.original: Pastores , hedera crescentem ornate poetam ,
sections.translation: in strains alternate — for alternate strains

sections.label: Section 7.26
sections.original: Arcades , invidia rumpantur ut ilia Codro ;
sections.translation: the Muses then were minded to recall —

sections.label: Section 7.27
sections.original: aut si ultra placitum laudarit , baccare frontem
sections.translation: first Corydon , then Thyrsis in reply .

sections.label: Section 7.28
sections.original: cingite , ne vati noceat mala lingua futuro .
sections.translation: “ Libethrian Nymphs , who are my heart ' s delight ,

sections.label: Section 7.29
sections.original: Saetosi caput hoc apri tibi , Delia , parvus
sections.translation: grant me , as doth my Codrus , so to sing —

sections.label: Section 7.30
sections.original: et ramosa Micon vivacis cornua cervi .
sections.translation: next to Apollo he — or if to this

sections.label: Section 8.1
sections.original: Pastorem Musam Damonis et Alphesiboei —
sections.translation: of Damon and Alphesiboeus now ,

sections.label: Section 8.2
sections.original: immemor herbarum quos est mirata iuvenca
sections.translation: those shepherd-singers at whose rival strains

sections.label: Section 8.3
sections.original: certantis , quorum stupefactae carmine lynces ,
sections.translation: the heifer wondering forgot to graze ,

sections.label: Section 8.4
sections.original: et mutata suos requierunt flumina cursus —
sections.translation: the lynx stood awe-struck , and the flowing streams ,

sections.label: Section 8.5
sections.original: Illonis Musam dicemus et Alphesiboei .
sections.translation: unwonted loiterers , stayed their course to hear —

sections.label: Section 8.6
sections.original: Tu mihi seu magni superas iam saxa Timavi ,
sections.translation: how Damon and Alphesiboeus sang

sections.label: Section 8.7
sections.original: sive oram Illyrici legis aequoris , en erit umquam
sections.translation: their pastoral ditties , will I tell the tale .

sections.label: Section 8.8
sections.original: ille dies , mihi cum liceat tua dicere facta ?
sections.translation: Thou , whether broad Timavus ' rocky banks

sections.label: Section 8.9
sections.original: en erit ut liceat totum mihi ferre per orbem
sections.translation: thou now art passing , or dost skirt the shore

sections.label: Section 8.10
sections.original: sola Sophocleo tua carmina digna coturno ?
sections.translation: of the Illyrian main ,— will ever dawn

sections.label: Section 8.11
sections.original: A te principium , tibi desinam : accipe iussis
sections.translation: that day when I thy deeds may celebrate ,

sections.label: Section 8.12
sections.original: carmina coepta tuis , atque hanc sine tempora circum
sections.translation: ever that day when through the whole wide world

sections.label: Section 8.13
sections.original: inter victrices hederam tibi serpere laurus .
sections.translation: I may renown thy verse — that verse alone

sections.label: Section 8.14
sections.original: Frigida vix caelo noctis decesserat umbra ,
sections.translation: of Sophoclean buskin worthy found ?

sections.label: Section 8.15
sections.original: cum ros in tenera pecori gratissimus herba ;
sections.translation: With thee began , to thee shall end , the strain .

sections.label: Section 8.16
sections.original: incumbens tereti Damon sic coepit olivae .
sections.translation: Take thou these songs that owe their birth to thee ,

sections.label: Section 8.17
sections.original: Nascere , praeque diem veniens age , Lucifer , almum ,
sections.translation: and deign around thy temples to let creep

sections.label: Section 8.18
sections.original: coniugis indigno Nisae deceptus amore
sections.translation: this ivy-chaplet ' twixt the conquering bays .

sections.label: Section 8.19
sections.original: dum queror , et divos , quamquam nil testibus illis
sections.translation: Scarce had night ' s chilly shade forsook the sky

sections.label: Section 8.20
sections.original: profeci , extrema moriens tamen adloquor hora .
sections.translation: what time to nibbling sheep the dewy grass

sections.label: Section 8.21
sections.original: Incipe Maenalios mecum , mea tibia , versus .
sections.translation: tastes sweetest , when , on his smooth shepherd-staff

sections.label: Section 8.22
sections.original: Maenalus argutumque nemus pinosque loquentis
sections.translation: of olive leaning , Damon thus began .

sections.label: Section 8.23
sections.original: semper habet ; semper pastorum ille audit amores ,
sections.translation: “ Rise , Lucifer , and , heralding the light ,

sections.label: Section 8.24
sections.original: Panaque , qui primus calamos non passus inertis .
sections.translation: bring in the genial day , while I make moan

sections.label: Section 8.25
sections.original: Incipe Maenalios mecum , mea tibia , versus .
sections.translation: fooled by vain passion for a faithless bride ,

sections.label: Section 8.26
sections.original: Mopso Nisa datur : quid non speremus amantes ?
sections.translation: for Nysa , and with this my dying breath

sections.label: Section 8.27
sections.original: Iungentur iam grypes equis , aevoque sequenti
sections.translation: call on the gods , though little it bestead —

sections.label: Section 8.28
sections.original: cum canibus timidi venient ad pocula dammae .
sections.translation: the gods who heard her vows and heeded not .

sections.label: Section 8.29
sections.original: Mopse , novas incide faces : tibi ducitur uxor ;
sections.translation: ‘ Begin , my flute , with me Maenalian lays .’

sections.label: Section 8.30
sections.original: sparge , marite , nuces : tibi deserit Hesperus Oetam .
sections.translation: Ever hath Maenalus his murmuring groves
